description: Since "wipe_secret_we" can only be true when "reg_error" is true,the HMAC secret wipe mechanism does not work under normal conditions.This allows an attacker to reuse the previous user's key to compute the HMAC digest.

The AES core is instantiated with SecAllowForcingMasks hard-wired to 1 (hw/ip/aes/rtl/aes.sv:L180), overriding the secure top-level default of 0 (L23¨CL25).This permanently enables the FORCE_MASKS CSR path, allowing software to force internal masks to constants and thereby disabling the DOM masking randomization intended to mitigate first-order side-channel leakage.By enabling constant mask forcing at run time, the design reintroduces first-order power/EM leakage in masked S-Box and key-addition operations. Any actor able to write the AES auxiliary control register can set FORCE_MASKS=1, run chosen AES operations, and perform standard first-order CPA/DPA to recover keys with modest traces. The condition is silent(no alerts), subverts the secure default, and cannot be disabled by SoC integration because the parameter is hard-overridden.
